{{Quote|They're trying to show us what valuable allies they'd make. Troops galore, a Super Star Destroyer'' […]|[[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]|<ref name="Destiny's Way" />}}
A '''Super Star Destroyer''' affiliated with the [[Imperial Remnant]] was present at the Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]] in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]], during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. Along with a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyers]], the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] patrolled [[Realspace/Legends|space]] above Bastion as a symbolic show of strength when [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] representatives [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] arrived there on a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] mission. Rather than landing directly on Bastion's surface, the New Republic delegates first boarded the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]], where they were escorted to a luxury model [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]], which then carried them to the planet below.

{{Ship-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Imperial Remnant Super Star Destroyer appeared briefly and without name in the [[2002]] novel [[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way|''Destiny's Way'']], written by [[Walter Jon Williams]] for [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series. ''Destiny's Way'' refers to the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] as an eight-[[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]] Super Star Destroyer,<ref name="Destiny's Way" /> a description that, at the time of the story's publication, corresponded exclusively to what eventually became known in ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] as the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]].<ref name="Note 2">At the time [[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way|''Destiny's Way'']] was published ([[October 1|October]] [[2002]]), ''[[Star Wars]]'' source material, including such publications as ''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]'' ([[December]] [[2001]]) and ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 47]]'' ([[November]] 2002), regularly referred to the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] as a [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] or [[Super-class Star Destroyer|''Super''-class Star Destroyer]] eight [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] in length. This specific combination of [[Starship/Legends|starship]] class and length never corresponded to any other ship class in [[canon]]. [[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']] ([[2005]]) eventually [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] the ''Super''-class Star Destroyer into the nineteen-kilometer ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought.</ref> The [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity ultimately [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] the ''Executor''-class from its original eight-kilometer length to be nineteen kilometers long and also established "Super Star Destroyer" as colloquial [[in-universe]] nomenclature to refer to multiple large [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] classes.<ref name="Essential Guide to Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> Despite this, [[Jason Fry]] concluded in his endnotes to ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', that any reference to a "Super Star Destroyer" in a novel is a reference to an ''Executor''-type ship unless there’s a very good reason to think otherwise.<ref>{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/23039847571/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-7|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 7}}</ref> Given the story's implicit intention, this article treats this Super Star Destroyer as an ''Executor''-class vessel.

==Appearances==
*''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' {{1st}}
